diagnosis: The remaining Wiley abstract gap is mixed gold, retrieval/cache, routing, and manual DOM work. The only tempting long-body parser change would violate the existing false-positive guard for article bodies with more than five paragraphs.

doi_evidence.notes: True Wiley page with no abstract section; div.article__body has 17 paragraphs, which the parser intentionally skips.
Only truncated metadata in cached HTML; no visible full abstract body.
Meta text is generic UI copy while current gold abstract is very short.
Cached page is the Wiley ePDF reader shell, not full article HTML.
Cochrane/alternate owner shape despite Wiley registrant bucket.

next_action: Route residual Wiley abstract rows to gold/retrieval/router/manual-DOM audit. Do not broaden Wiley article-body fallback without a repeated safe selector cluster.

tests_run.command: read-only sidecar artifact inspection plus focused cached-HTML probes
tests_run.result: No repeated safe Wiley-parser-owned selector pattern found.
